item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just have to say that I appreciate and love the swath this article cuts through the four different canons it's set in. I can see each canon leaking into this in different ways. And using an Artificial Intelligence in the place of a human soul for sacrificial purposes is ingenious.

I read this before but I don't think I truly ever appreciated how awesome it is. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love how this complements the Rogue AI GoI format. I was hooked all the way through. Nice work.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Man I been wondering when somebody was gonna notice that.

Edit: Little bit of worldbuilding of the Journeymen, which is a GOI based around stealing shit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
None of y'all gonna mention the auctioneer being named Jack Sheppard? No?

Okay then.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I almost cried when I saw it got stolen , then let out a breath when I realized the Foundation got the little AI back. Man… I was worried for a second..

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

This one already has some pretty solid links to it, so I'm going to keep my suggestions simple. The mentions of MC&D, Anderson Robotics, and Kappa-10 can be turned into links to the MC&D Hub, AR Hub, and Kappa-10 MTF Page respectively. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

Current data recovered from Marshall Carter & Dark indicate that SCP-2987-1 was created for the sole purpose of use in an exchange with an extradimensional entity.

To this:

Current data recovered from Marshall Carter & Dark indicate that SCP-2987-1 was created for the sole purpose of use in an exchange with an extradimensional entity.

Changing this:

SCP-2987's existence had been noted following its sale by Anderson Robotics in February 2014. However, the SUSEOCT prevented direct attempts to acquire the object from Marshall Carter & Dark.

To this:

SCP-2987's existence had been noted following its sale by Anderson Robotics in February 2014. However, the SUSEOCT prevented direct attempts to acquire the object from Marshall Carter & Dark.

Changing this:

On May 18th MTF Kappa-10 "Skynet" was activated and deployed in the gathering of intelligence regarding the re-acquisition of both SCP-2987 and SCP-2987-1. The auction listing above was retrieved during this time.

To this:

On May 18th MTF Kappa-10 "Skynet" was activated and deployed in the gathering of intelligence regarding the re-acquisition of both SCP-2987 and SCP-2987-1. The auction listing above was retrieved during this time.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I sincerely hope more work is done on this. Not because I don't like it, but because I absolutely love the way it's going. Personally, I'd like to see more of SCP 2987-1, but mostly because I liked the way the character was going.

I'm going to go hit the upvote button now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 from me. The skip is ostensibly just the hard drive, whereas the AI seems almost "pedestrian" (in the world of the Foundation, anyway) but is made relevant by the hard drive and the whole thing unfolds well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A great concept executed quite well in a canon that I very much enjoy. I feel like the direction of "spooky applications of advanced science" is a far more fruitful direction for Anderson Robotics articles than "advanced robots/AIs." Heartily +1'd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty old, but I can see this being connected to SCP-2791, there's a similar idea going on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Following some events involving MTF Kappa-10… this has been updated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Here's the line that sold me:

Warning: Do not attempt to use the item in any ritual that requires bloodletting from the offering. We are not responsible for deaths which result from such misuse of the item.

I can just see someone threatening to sue MC&D over a botched ritual despite the clear instructions.

I can also see some beings refusing to accept an artificial soul for their own reasons, even though it technically meets the requirements. Were I an evil god of some sort, I might be offended if my ritual required sacrificing a child and someone tried to weasel out of it. After all, I'm evil! I want my supplicants to show some commitment to evilness! Kids these days got no respect for the old ways, it's all smartphones and ebooks and artificial souls. GET OFF MY LAWN.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was answered up thread, but I'll answer it again here (as that was a little while ago):

This document is still in place because in-story it was stolen pretty much a day or two ago. It will be revised, but there's not been time yet for debriefing. Also they're still mulling if they wanna make a bid for the AI in the auction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait… if it was stolen, shouldn't be 'Uncontained' or something like that, instead of Safe?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Question: can the drive convert the AI back into being an AI once it has been soulified?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm so fucking happy I finally got around to read this.

Alright its a neutral vote for me, mostly because while I think its somewhat of a good idea, I just found the article a little hard to understand at times. It's cool that Alexander is related to the whole AIAD thingamajig, but i just had a hard time following it.

I also got confused at the end where MC&D sold Alexander away again. I think that was a little pointless as they saved Alexander from MC&D the first time.

I do like the idea tho.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The idea is excellent and the execution is more than sufficient, but I have one or two little issues with the tone. I don't know how to describe it, exactly, but the A.I. sounds almost a little TOO much like a robot. "That is desirable," right at the end, set off my cornometer.

Still definitely a +1, of course.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Concurring with this. Well written, but not doing much for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't mind this at all.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really loved this. Great concept executed well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, that's clear now. I think I misread some dialog in one your Resurrection pieces. Dietrich Lurk is a totally fine name. My apologies!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A lot of standard naming conventions for personnel, especially in militaries, are usually LAST NAME, FIRST NAME abbrv. Like Smith J., Holmes F., Torres I., Lurk D. for example.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I completely and utterly love the concept and wish I had thought of it first.
The auction listing makes it that much better - custom made AI personalities to best appease requesting entity are a lovely bit of detail.
Wish I could upvote twice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mmmmm… it was more a question of it being a grand scheme versus clever opportunizing on the part of MC&D in response to unfolding events.

I got the second option on the first read-through.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Augh no ow ow yes.

Er, that is, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was actually the point of it. Did I bury the tweest? ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That makes sense, I suppose. Changing to upvote, also partially because I just realized that this whole thing could have been a ploy by MC&D for to have a legitimately rebellious AI/soul.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Lots of stuff going on in here, and it all works great. My first thought upon reading the purpose of the item was "How useful! And how great that demon summoning has finally entered the computer age!" And then you went ahead and gave the AI a personality, and that boots this up into excellent, what with the pining for his fate at the end. Nicely done!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Glad this got off the ground after all, Cimmerian, for more reasons than just the Anderson reference. I really liked the MC&D documentation at the end, and the bit about Anderson's offices depositing the raid team in North Korea was pretty neat.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When I was trying to think of a name for this, I almost went with "Electric Sheep" until I realized there's already one named that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was not intentional and is now fixed. Thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I guess androids do dream of electric sheep. +1'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2987-1: To discussed our purpose. Our goals.

Is this intentional grammar?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This document is still in place because in story it was stolen pretty much a day or two ago. It would be revised, but there's not been time yet for debriefing. Also they're still mulling if they wanna make a bid for the AI in the auction. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted hard. Was expecting an experiment log but got something much better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked it a lot. I suspect that's partially for the references to canon bits I enjoy, so I'm tempering myself a bit there. Ultimately, I also feel it very good in most parts, but fell a little short of its ultimate potential.

I'm temporarily no-voting, but not because of that. I'm mostly not sure how I feel about the documentation of an SCP that has been stolen and is no longer in containment. There's no mention of this in the Containment Procedures. I don't know if those would necessarily have to be modified, but it seems like an oversight. (I also understand why you would want to keep that order for narrative purposes, but it still feels off to me.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a little bit flat, and it doesn't take me for a ride the way that the best articles on this site do, but there's something ingeniously practical about manufacturing souls for the purpose of trade that rubs me the right way. It seems very in-character for MC&D, and I'm upvoting based on the strength of concept at work here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought it was Lurk Dietrich and not the other way. I guess it is less odd a name than I thought. My apologies to LurkD for poor reading comprehension.

Also this is great! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's an interesting concept, but it doesn't do enough with the idea to retain that interest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fantastic work. An intriguing concept and several addenda that serve to explain without taking away the inherent mystery. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wanna give him a hug.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love it, love it, love it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's almost there, but the interview falls flat for me and there was never really a moment where everything clicked together and worked for me. It's a neat object, and written well, but it didn't really add up and pay off at the end. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Lotta folk helped me shine this up. Decibelle, LurkD, Vezaz, Conwell, Dmatix, Zyn… I'm forgetting some folk maybe but thanks.

This is firmly in The Gulf canon, for the record. Image is something I cooked up in MSpaint.

As for the title…

+ show block
– hide block

Out of the night that covers me,
Black as the pit from pole to pole,
I thank whatever gods may be
For my unconquerable soul.

In the fell clutch of circumstance
I have not winced nor cried aloud.
Under the bludgeonings of chance
My head is bloody, but unbowed.

Beyond this place of wrath and tears
Looms but the Horror of the shade,
And yet the menace of the years
Finds, and shall find me, unafraid.

It matters not how strait the gate,
How charged with punishments the scroll,
I am the master of my fate:
I am the captain of my soul.

~ Invictus by William Ernest Henley
